fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Given a truncation level n, we define what it means for a type to be n-truncated by induction:

fr:ul.fr:li: a type is -2-truncated when it is contractible;
a type is -1-truncated when it is a proposition;
for n \geq 0, a type A is n-truncated when for every x,y:A the identification type x=y is n-1-truncated.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: So, in particular, a 0-truncated type is precisely a set. We shall often refer to n-truncated types as n-types.

A type A is said to be contractible with centre a:A when every x:A is equipped with an identification \chi _x\colon a = x. We say that A is contractible when it comes equipped with some a:A such that A is contractible with centre a. We sometimes refer to a contractible type as a singleton.
A type A is said to be a proposition when every x:A, the type A is contractible with centre x, i.e. all elements of A are identified.
A type A is called a set when for each x,y:A the identification type x=y is a proposition.
The notions of contractible and propositional types generate an infinite hierarchy of truncation levels expressing the point after which higher identifications become trivial.
A truncation level is an integer n \geq -2.
Given a truncation level n, we define what it means for a type to be n-truncated by induction:

fr:ul.fr:li: a type is -2-truncated when it is contractible;
a type is -1-truncated when it is a proposition;
for n \geq 0, a type A is n-truncated when for every x,y:A the identification type x=y is n-1-truncated.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: So, in particular, a 0-truncated type is precisely a set. We shall often refer to n-truncated types as n-types.

A function f\colon A \to B is called n-truncated when all of its fibers are n-truncated types. By analogy, we shall also refer to maps as contractible when their fibers are; we shall refer to maps as propositional when their fibers are propositions.

fr:title.text: n-truncated types are closed under retracts ›

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: n-truncated types are closed under retracts ›

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: By induction on n; the base cases for n=-2 and n=-1 are given by Corollary and Lemma respectively. In the inductive step, we assume that s\colon B\to A exhibits B as a retract of A and that for each x,y:A the identification type x=_Ay is n+1-truncated to show that for all u,v:B the identification type u=_Bv is likewise n+1-truncated. The result follows from our assumption and the fact that u=_Bv is (by Lemma ) a retract of su=_Asv.
If A is n+1-truncated, then for each x,y:A the identification type x=y is n-truncated.
By induction on n; the base case for n=-2 is given by Corollary , and the other cases are trivial.

fr:title.text: n-truncated types are closed under retracts

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: n-truncated types are closed under retracts

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Any retract of an n-truncated type is n-truncated.

By induction on n; the base cases for n=-2 and n=-1 are given by Corollary and Lemma respectively. In the inductive step, we assume that s\colon B\to A exhibits B as a retract of A and that for each x,y:A the identification type x=_Ay is n+1-truncated to show that for all u,v:B the identification type u=_Bv is likewise n+1-truncated. The result follows from our assumption and the fact that u=_Bv is (by Lemma ) a retract of su=_Asv.
If a type A is n-truncated, then it is also n+1-truncated.
By induction on the truncation level n, with two base cases given by Lemma and Lemma .

fr:title.text: Being an n-truncated map is a proposition ›

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Being an n-truncated map is a proposition ›

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: It is a proposition that a given type A is n-truncated.
We proceed by induction on the truncation level n.

fr:ol.fr:li: When n=-2, we recall from Lemma that being contractible is a proposition.
When n=-1, we recall from Lemma that being a proposition is a proposition.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A function f\colon A \to B is called n-truncated when all of its fibers are n-truncated types. By analogy, we shall also refer to maps as contractible when their fibers are; we shall refer to maps as propositional when their fibers are propositions.

A type A is said to be contractible with centre a:A when every x:A is equipped with an identification \chi _x\colon a = x. We say that A is contractible when it comes equipped with some a:A such that A is contractible with centre a. We sometimes refer to a contractible type as a singleton.
A truncation level is an integer n \geq -2.
A type A is said to be a proposition when every x:A, the type A is contractible with centre x, i.e. all elements of A are identified.
